---
title: "Gilt Retention in Commercial Herds — 2025 International Pig Livability Conference"
description: |
  Presenting our findings on factors associated with gilt retention at the 2025 International Pig Livability Conference — and why reproductive management and herd environment matter for sow longevity.
author: "Elly Kirwa"
date: "2025-12-01"
categories: [Swine Production, Gilt Retention, Sow Longevity, Research]
sidebar: false
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
---

I had the opportunity to present part of our work at the **2025 International Pig Livability Conference**, focusing on factors associated with gilt retention in commercial breeding herds.

## Key Findings

Our findings highlight the combined impact of reproductive management and herd environment on sow longevity. Optimizing gilt entry conditions — particularly PRRSV stability, age at first service, and breeding site — offers practical strategies to enhance productivity and extend productive sow life.

## The Bigger Picture

Gilt retention is not just a biological outcome — it is an economic one. Every gilt that fails to reach her second or third parity represents a significant replacement cost and lost productivity potential. By identifying the predictors of retention early, producers can intervene with the right management strategies at the right time.

## Industry Collaboration

The robust exchange of ideas with producers, researchers, and industry teams was the ultimate highlight of the conference, underscoring the collaborative nature of the swine industry.
